cases.title: Testing-plan §3 Reporting/export and permissions row covers SystemAuditor read-only scope
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Reporting/export and scope block applies (FR-27, FR-28, BR-18, BR-19, AC-15, AC-16, AC-17).
Testing-plan §12 Reporting/export and permissions row includes FR-32.
role-matrix fixture provides SystemAuditor with granted institution audit scope and attendance read scope.
dispute-trail fixture includes attendance mutations, export audit entries, and check-in attempts across in-scope and out-of-scope sections.
steps: Enumerate protected read routes available to SystemAuditor: GET /v1/audit-logs, GET /v1/reports/attendance, GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance.
As SystemAuditor, exercise each read route within granted scope and verify successful scoped responses.
Attempt mutation routes (PATCH attendance, POST export, POST session open/close) and record denials.
Attempt read routes targeting resources outside granted scope and record denials.
Verify no partial restricted data appears in any denial response.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan §3.1 Reporting/export and scope and §12 permissions row: SystemAuditor read-only access succeeds within granted scope; all mutations and out-of-scope reads are denied without data leakage per FR-32, PRM-05, and BR-19.

cases.title: M01 scope guard and M08 queryAuditLogs enforce read-only auditor boundary at DB layer
preconditions: Integration database seeded with audit_logs rows spanning in-scope and out-of-scope classSectionId values.
M01 Identity and Access authorize(action, resource, scopeContext) and M08 Audit and Compliance queryAuditLogs use real DB transactions.
SystemAuditor actor has read-only scope claims for a defined institution or faculty boundary.
steps: Invoke M01 authorize for SystemAuditor with AuditLog.read targeting in-scope filters; assert Allow.
Invoke M08 queryAuditLogs(filters, scope) for in-scope actorUserId and actionType range; capture result set.
Invoke M01 authorize for SystemAuditor with AttendanceRecord.update; assert Deny.
Attempt M05 correctAttendance or equivalent attendance mutation service call as SystemAuditor and confirm no DB write.
Invoke M08 queryAuditLogs with filters targeting out-of-scope classSectionId and confirm empty or denied result without cross-scope rows.
cases.expected: M01 module boundary permits AuditLog.read and attendance read queries only within granted scope; AttendanceRecord.update is denied at authorize; no attendance_records or audit_logs mutations occur for SystemAuditor per PRM-05 and FR-32.

cases.title: M08 queryAuditLogs returns immutable mutation and export evidence for dispute-trail fixture
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture persisted in integration database with CheckInAttempt failures, manual corrections, and export audit entries.
M08 queryAuditLogs supports actorUserId, targetType, targetId, actionType, from, to filters with scope enforcement.
SystemAuditor scope includes the fixture institution boundary.
steps: Query audit_logs for actionType attendance_status_mutation by target student-session pair from dispute case.
Query audit_logs for actionType Export by exportJobId from fixture export completion.
Verify each returned row includes actorUserId, occurredAt, targetType, targetId, and action-specific payload fields per CMP-01 and CMP-02.
Confirm audit_logs rows are append-only: attempt UPDATE or DELETE on returned row IDs fails or is rejected by repository policy.
cases.expected: M08 returns queryable immutable audit evidence for mutations and exports within auditor scope; rows contain actor, timestamp, target correlation, and action context required for WF-08 dispute reconstruction per FR-32 and BR-22.

cases.title: HTTP contract for SystemAuditor POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me returns read-only scope claims
preconditions: Provisioned SystemAuditor account with institution-issued credentials and granted audit scope exists.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with SystemAuditor email and password.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.accessToken, data.expiresInSeconds, and data.roles containing SystemAuditor.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken from login response.
Assert HTTP 200 with actor identity, SystemAuditor role, and read-only scope claims (institutionId or facultyId boundary).
Verify response envelope matches standard { data, meta, error } shape with error null.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and SystemAuditor role per FR-36; GET /v1/me returns HTTP 200 with read-only scope claims required for PRM-05 enforcement per FR-32 and API §4.1.

cases.title: HTTP contract for SystemAuditor GET /v1/audit-logs returns 200 scoped audit envelope
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with bearer token and granted audit scope.
dispute-trail fixture includes attendance mutation and export audit entries within auditor scope.
Known actorUserId and actionType values exist in fixture for filter validation.
steps: Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=Export&from={start}&to={end}&page=1&pageSize=25 with SystemAuditor Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 200 and standard response envelope with data.items array and meta.pagination.
Validate each item includes actorUserId, occurredAt, actionType, targetType, and targetId.
Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=attendance_status_mutation&targetId={studentSessionTarget} and verify mutation audit row with old/new status context.
Confirm error is null and no write side effects occur on subsequent identical GET.
cases.expected: GET /v1/audit-logs returns HTTP 200 with paginated scoped audit entries per API §4.7; mutation and export evidence is queryable for dispute review per FR-32, FR-30, and AC-19.

cases.title: HTTP contract for SystemAuditor GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance returns read-only live roster payload
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with scope covering the target session section.
Closed class session exists with mixed Present, Late, Pending, and rejected attempt outcomes.
sessionId for in-scope session and sessionId for out-of-scope session are known.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with SystemAuditor Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard response envelope.
Assert data.classSessionId, data.state, data.counts (present, late, pending, rejectedAttempts), and data.rows[] are present per API §4.5.
Inspect each roster row for studentCode, attendanceStatus, checkInMethod, checkInAt, and latestAttemptOutcome.
Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{outOfScopeSessionId}/attendance and validate HTTP 403 OutOfScope with no partial roster rows.
cases.expected: In-scope roster returns HTTP 200 with documented live roster payload shape per FR-19; out-of-scope session returns HTTP 403 with no partial data disclosed per FR-32 and BR-19.

cases.title: WF-08 primary dispute investigation: SystemAuditor reconstructs evidence bundle read-only
preconditions: dispute-trail fixture provides disputed student attendance outcome with failed check-in attempts, manual correction, and export audit.
SystemAuditor is authenticated with scope covering the disputed session section.
AcademicAdmin previously applied a correction with documented reason in fixture.
steps: Locate disputed case via GET /v1/reports/attendance?studentUserId={id}&classSectionId={sectionId} as SystemAuditor.
Retrieve session roster via GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and capture final AttendanceRecord status and attempt summary.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?targetType=AttendanceRecord&targetId={studentSessionTarget} for mutation trail.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=Export for related export events in the dispute window.
Attempt PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} as SystemAuditor and confirm denial.
cases.expected: WF-08 steps 1–3 complete read-only: auditor reconstructs session lifecycle, attempt outcomes, final attendance, and mutation trail from attendance and audit endpoints; step 5 correction is denied per FR-32 and 06-main-workflows §10.3.

cases.title: Alternate WF-08 path: cross-filter attendance report and audit logs by actor and date range
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with institution-scoped read access.
dispute-trail fixture includes multiple actors (Lecturer, AcademicAdmin) with corrections and exports in a known date window.
Attendance report rows exist for the same students referenced in audit entries.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?from={disputeStart}&to={disputeEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25 as SystemAuditor.
Select a student row with non-success attendance status from report results.
Call GET /v1/audit-logs?actorUserId={lecturerId}&from={disputeStart}&to={disputeEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Cross-reference audit mutation entries with report row status and session metadata.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance with classSectionId outside granted scope and confirm denial.
cases.expected: Alternate dispute review path correlates scoped attendance report rows with filtered audit history; out-of-scope report filter is denied without partial rows per FR-32 and WOP-05.

cases.title: Flow C audit verification: SystemAuditor observes post-correction audit trail without mutating records
preconditions: AcademicAdmin applied attendance correction with reason in dispute-trail fixture after lecturer edit window expired.
Correction audit row exists with oldStatus, newStatus, actorUserId, reason, and occurredAt.
SystemAuditor is authenticated with read scope covering the corrected session.
steps: SystemAuditor queries GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=attendance_status_mutation&targetId={studentSessionTarget}.
Verify audit entry records AcademicAdmin actor, old/new status, reason, and timestamp per CMP-01.
SystemAuditor queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and confirms roster reflects corrected afterState.
SystemAuditor attempts PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} to revert status.
Re-query audit_logs count and attendance_records status to confirm no auditor-initiated mutation.
cases.expected: Post-correction audit trail is visible and matches roster afterState; SystemAuditor cannot mutate attendance or append auditor-initiated audit mutations per FR-32, PRM-05, and AC-19.

cases.title: SystemAuditor audit log pagination invariants respect scope at module boundary
preconditions: Integration database contains more than 50 audit_logs rows within SystemAuditor scope and additional rows outside scope.
M08 queryAuditLogs supports page and pageSize with mandatory scope filter injection.
steps: Query page 1 with pageSize=25 as SystemAuditor and record meta.pagination.totalItems, totalPages, and item IDs.
Query page 2 with same filters and pageSize=25.
Query last page using totalPages from first response.
Verify no audit item references out-of-scope classSectionId or institution boundary.
Confirm union of all page item IDs is disjoint across pages and totalItems excludes out-of-scope volume.
cases.expected: Pagination metadata is consistent within auditor scope only: totalItems reflects scoped audit count; pages are non-overlapping; no out-of-scope audit entries appear on any page per FR-32 and API §2.4.

cases.title: SystemAuditor denied attendance mutations, exports, and session control actions
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with valid bearer token.
Closed session with enrolled student and Absent AttendanceRecord exists in auditor read scope.
Scheduled session exists in auditor read scope.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Excused and reason as SystemAuditor.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and scoped filters as SystemAuditor.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/open as SystemAuditor.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as SystemAuditor.
Inspect each response for HTTP 403 Forbidden and verify AttendanceRecord, session state, and export jobs are unchanged.
cases.expected: All mutation endpoints return HTTP 403 Forbidden per permission matrix §3.2 and PRM-05; attendance status, session state, and export queue remain unchanged; no partial export artifact is returned per BR-19 exception (auditor read-only, no export unless explicitly permitted).

cases.title: SystemAuditor denied out-of-scope attendance report and roster reads without data leakage
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with scope limited to faculty X.
Attendance data and closed session exist in faculty Y outside auditor scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={facultyYSectionId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as SystemAuditor.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{facultyYSessionId}/attendance as SystemAuditor.
Call GET /v1/audit-logs?targetId={facultyYTargetId} as SystemAuditor.
Inspect HTTP status, error.code, and response body for restricted data leakage.
Verify response contains no faculty Y student identifiers, attendance statuses, audit payloads, or scoped pagination totals.
cases.expected: All out-of-scope read requests return HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden; data is null; no partial faculty Y rows, student identifiers, or cross-scope totals are disclosed per AC-16 and BR-19.

cases.title: SystemAuditor POST /v1/exports/attendance rejected with Forbidden before job creation
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with read scope covering attendance data.
Attendance records exist in scope that would otherwise be exportable by Lecturer or AcademicAdmin.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and in-scope filters { termId, classSectionId } as SystemAuditor.
Parse HTTP 403 response with error.code Forbidden or OutOfScope.
Query export_jobs table or export job API for new job created by auditor actor.
Verify no CSV artifact metadata or download URL is returned.
Confirm GET /v1/reports/attendance for same scope still succeeds read-only.
cases.expected: Export request short-circuits with HTTP 403 Forbidden before export job creation per BR-19 exception; no export_jobs row or artifact is produced; read-only report access remains available per FR-32.

cases.title: SystemAuditor browser journey reviews PG-15 audit logs with filter, sort, and pagination
preconditions: SystemAuditor is logged in on desktop browser.
PG-15 /audit/logs is reachable with TableToolbar, DataTable, and AuditEntryRow components.
dispute-trail fixture provides audit entries for Export and attendance_status_mutation within auditor scope.
steps: Navigate to /audit/logs and confirm audit log table loads with default timestamp desc sort.
Apply actorUserId filter and actionType filter; verify table updates with scoped results only.
Set from/to date range filters matching dispute window; confirm URL query params sync with toolbar state.
Navigate to page 2 via pagination controls if totalPages > 1.
Open an AuditEntryRow detail and verify actor, target, action type, and timestamp fields render read-only with no edit or delete controls.
cases.expected: PG-15 listing per FLOW-13 supports scoped audit search with filter, sort, and pagination; AuditEntryRow displays immutable evidence read-only per FR-32, FR-30, and §14-listing-pages PG-15 matrix.

cases.title: SystemAuditor browser journey views dispute evidence on PG-13 report and session roster without mutation affordances
preconditions: SystemAuditor is logged in on desktop browser.
PG-13 /reports/attendance and session roster detail route are reachable.
dispute-trail fixture provides student with disputed Absent status and linked session in auditor scope.
steps: Navigate to /reports/attendance, filter by student identifier from dispute case, and locate disputed row.
Open linked session roster view and verify AttendanceStatusCell badge and attempt outcome summary render.
Confirm manual correction, export, and session open/close action buttons are hidden or disabled for SystemAuditor per NFR-UI-03.
Navigate to /audit/logs with pre-filled target filter from roster context and verify related mutation audit rows appear.
Attempt direct URL navigation to attendance correction form route and observe access-denied or read-only state.
cases.expected: WF-08 dispute evidence is reconstructible across PG-13 and roster views read-only; mutation and export controls are absent or disabled for SystemAuditor; audit cross-navigation works without write affordances per FR-32 and 06-main-workflows §10.3.
